items.headline: Fans 'set the standards' at rocking Emirates: Arteta
items.description: Mikel Arteta said that Arsenal fans "set the standards" after the Gunners reached the Champions League final with a 2-1 aggregate win...

items.headline: Marlins designate veteran pitcher Paddack for assignment
items.description:
MIAMI (AP) — The Miami Marlins designated pitcher Chris Paddack for assignment on Tuesday, ending the veteran right-hander’s brief stint with the club.
The 30-year-old Paddack signed a one-year, $4 million free agent deal with the Marlins in the offseason and was targeted for an end-of-the rotation role. But Paddack struggled in his seven starts, posting an 0-5 record and 7.63 ERA. Paddack completed six innings in only one of his appearances.

items.headline: Astros lose catcher Yainer Diaz 'for a while' because of a left oblique strain
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Houston catcher Yainer Diaz was placed on the 10-day injured list with a left oblique strain Tuesday.
Diaz was injured in batting practice Monday and manager Joe Espada didn’t provide a specific timeline for his injury but said he’d be out for "a while.”

items.description: Key quotes after Arsenal beat Atletico Madrid 2-1 on aggregate to reach the...

items.headline: The Knicks are the East favorites in the sportsbooks and suddenly look like it on the court
items.description:
GREENBURGH, N.Y. (AP) — The New York Knicks are now the favorites to win the Eastern Conference in the sportsbooks and they sure look like it on the court.
The first team to win three straight postseason games by at least 25 points is looking more and more like the one that could make New York’s first NBA Finals appearance in the 2000s.

items.headline: Saka ends Arsenal's 20-year wait to reach Champions League final
items.description: Arsenal reached the Champions League final for the first time in 20 years as Bukayo Saka sealed a 1-0 win...

items.headline: WNBA general managers vote A'ja Wilson as the league's likely repeat MVP
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — WNBA general managers voted Las Vegas Aces center A’ja Wilson as the favorite to be named the league’s Most Valuable Player, according to an annual survey released Tuesday.
The Aces were also voted the favorites to repeat as league champions, according to the survey of all 15 general managers.

items.headline: Arsenal beat Atletico Madrid to reach Champions League final
items.description: Bukayo Saka scored the only goal of the game as Arsenal beat Atletico Madrid 1-0 on Tuesday to progress to...

items.headline: LIV Golf had a blank canvas. The PGA Tour could use one as it tries to solve a packed schedule
items.description:
LIV Golf had a seemingly endless supply of Saudi money that suddenly is coming to an end. Overlooked is another of its assets — this one with no price tag — the PGA Tour would love to have.
A blank canvas.

items.headline: Giants are signing defensive tackle D.J. Reader, AP source says
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — The New York Giants are signing defensive tackle D.J. Reader to a two-year contract worth $12.5 million, according to a person familiar with the move.
The person spoke to The Associated Press on condition of anonymity Tuesday because the deal had not been announced.
